children.value: A comprehensive reference guide for trigonometry, algebra, linear equations, and discrete mathematics.
The fundamental trigonometric ratios relate angles in a right triangle to the sides:
sin(θ) = Opposite / Hypotenuse    |    cos(θ) = Adjacent / Hypotenuse    |    tan(θ) = Opposite / Adjacent

children.html: sin⁡(θ)=OppositeHypotenuse\sin(\theta) = \frac{\text{Opposite}}{\text{Hypotenuse}}sin(θ)=HypotenuseOpposite​
cos⁡(θ)=AdjacentHypotenuse\cos(\theta) = \frac{\text{Adjacent}}{\text{Hypotenuse}}cos(θ)=HypotenuseAdjacent​
tan⁡(θ)=OppositeAdjacent\tan(\theta) = \frac{\text{Opposite}}{\text{Adjacent}}tan(θ)=AdjacentOpposite​
90°90°90°

cot⁡(θ)=1tan⁡(θ)sec⁡(θ)=1cos⁡(θ)csc⁡(θ)=1sin⁡(θ)\cot(\theta) = \frac{1}{\tan(\theta)} \quad \sec(\theta) = \frac{1}{\cos(\theta)} \quad \csc(\theta) = \frac{1}{\sin(\theta)}cot(θ)=tan(θ)1​sec(θ)=cos(θ)1​csc(θ)=sin(θ)1​

sin⁡(A−B)=sin⁡Acos⁡B−cos⁡Asin⁡B\sin(A-B) = \sin A\cos B - \cos A\sin Bsin(A−B)=sinAcosB−cosAsinB
cos⁡(A+B)=cos⁡Acos⁡B−sin⁡Asin⁡B\cos(A+B) = \cos A\cos B - \sin A\sin Bcos(A+B)=cosAcosB−sinAsinB
cos⁡(A−B)=cos⁡Acos⁡B+sin⁡Asin⁡B\cos(A-B) = \cos A\cos B + \sin A\sin Bcos(A−B)=cosAcosB+sinAsinB

tan⁡(A−B)=tan⁡A−tan⁡B1+tan⁡Atan⁡B\tan(A-B) = \frac{\tan A - \tan B}{1 + \tan A\tan B}tan(A−B)=1+tanAtanBtanA−tanB​
